MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jul-30-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Suzanne Schornack, Esq. 510-409-8182 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Nils Rosenquest, Esq. 415-292-0980 Rosenquest & Associates for Defendant.
Court reporter: Tamara Willat, CSR #4609, 415-272-1753, tamarawillat@sbc.global.net, reported.

ARGUED. Ms. Schornack argues 3 points: Defendant is not entitled to equittable relief due to uncleanhands, papers are not filed correctly and requests that Defendant file his answer only. Mr. Rosenquest apprises the Court and responds to arguments. the court indicates he did not see Defendant's declaration. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TOURAJ BOUSSINA's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND.

GRANTED. Defendant to file a response no later than 5 days of notice of entry of order. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Evidence Code 452(d). (The Court notes that Defendant's attorney did not file a declaration under CCP 473(b).

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Tamara Willat, CSR #4609, 415-272-1753, tamarawillat@sbc.global.net, reported. (501/cfh)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-02-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

David P. Wasserman; 415-567-9600; appearing as counsel for defendants Brett Gibbs and Nils Rosenquest.

Plaintiff contests the following portions of the court's tentative decision:

"Defendants' Motion to Strike paragraph 21 of the Complaint is DENIED. The Complaint is so uncertain as to the dates of the events in question it is impossible to determine if CCP Section 425.16(e)(2) applies."

"Defendants' Motion to Strike subsection 7 of paragraph 47 of the Complaint is DENIED. The Complaint is so uncertain as to the dates of the events in question it is impossible to determine if CCP Section 425.16(e)(2) applies."

Plaintiff submits to the remainder of the court's tentative ruling. After hearing oral arguments, the court adopts the uncontested portions of it's tentative ruling, and takes the two contested portions under submission. Counsel is instructed to prepare an order conforming to the court's tentative ruling.

The court rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS' SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is taken under submission.
